title: TROLL Profiles Quick Start
text: NOTE: example uses stn_secs = 24 included dataset short stationary periods. Figure 1: Example output showing compiled profile data BGA (RFU). results expected: Missing depths –> decrease stn_secs Noisy non-optical parameter data –> adjust stbl_range_thresholds Want inspect behavior –> rerun plot = TRUE control, see Tuning Troll Workflow vignette.
code: library(ImportUtils)  # Example data (replace with your own file path) path_to_raw <- system.file("extdata", "TROLL_exdata.csv", package = "ImportUtils")  ## Run the full workflow dat <- TROLL_profile_compiler(   path = path_to_raw,    stn_secs = 24      # adjusted for this dataset )  # View summarized output summary <- dat$Summary_Data summary #> # A tibble: 9 × 8 #>   stationary_depth sp_conductivity_uScm temperature_C pH_units DO_mgL #>              <dbl>                <dbl>         <dbl>    <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1            0.085                 66.8         18.0      7.26   9.85 #> 2            1.04                  66.6         18.1      7.41   9.86 #> 3            1.94                  66.3         17.5      7.33   9.68 #> 4            2.90                  66.7         16.1      7.00   9.09 #> 5            3.91                  65.7         15.3      6.74   8.02 #> 6            4.88                  67.2         12.7      6.53   6.43 #> 7            5.84                  66.9         10.6      6.36   5.08 #> 8            6.82                  67.0          9.83     6.12   4.05 #> 9            7.68                  68.9          9.52     5.84   2.38 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: chlorophyll_RFU <dbl>, turbidity_NTU <dbl>, #> #   bga_fluorescence_RFU <dbl>

title: TROLL Profiles Quick Start
text: AquaTROLL utility functions ImportUtils provide tools processing electronic logs generated VuSitu app. primary workhorse TROLL_profile_compiler(), wraps functionality supporting functions single interface batch processing. component function used within TROLL_profile_compiler() also exported individually. allows users inspect intermediate steps, troubleshoot issues, better understand data transformed throughout workflow.workflow : Read Raw Data (TROLL_read_data()) Standardize column names required data (TROLL_rename_cols()) Detect flag stationary periods sonde data (is_stationary()) Detect flag stable data sensor type (TROLL_sensor_stable()) Summarize stationary + stable data (TROLL_summary_stable()) functions assume raw VuSitu .html files already converted .csv format. vignette walks complete example workflow using individual functions, followed wrapper function. goal demonstrate purpose step help users make informed decisions efficient accurate data processing.

title: TROLL Profiles Quick Start
text: TROLL_read_data() function reads raw .csv files exported VuSitu. uses pattern matching locate “Date Time” column determine data begin. function checks whether seconds included datetime values attempts standardize formatting needed. “Date Time” column used identify start dataset, must present input file. stage, data imported without interpretation modification beyond basic formatting. Note format Date Time Depth (m) columns.
code: path <- system.file(   "extdata",   "TROLL_exdata.csv",   package = "ImportUtils" ) dat_read <- TROLL_read_data(path ) # dplyr::glimpse(dat_read)

title: TROLL Profiles Quick Start
text: Downstream functions rely column names named consistently; function enforces standard (canonical) naming conventions via troll_column_dictionary. Column renaming achieved pattern matching. unknown columns detected error displayed dictionary updated columns represent new sensor data. possible work around missing names creating unique dictionary object passing colname_dictionary argument using troll_column_dictionary template adding additional rows required. Columns associated TROLL COMM automatically detected using internal dataset Comm serial numbers. Comm temperature column renamed avoid conflicts water temperature. Comm columns stripped away metadata whent strip_metadata = TRUE. trollcomm_serials arguments allows users use custom vector serial numbers associated Troll-Comm units, internal dataset date. Metadata columns (e.g. “latitude”, “longitude”, “battery” etc.) removed default, can retained toggling strip_metadata == FALSE. inclusive list metadata columns defined within column dictionary: troubleshooting, verbose argument may toggled allow printing messages console regarding presence/absense Troll-Comm columns, parameters detected.
code: dat_rename <- TROLL_rename_cols(   df = dat_read,   colname_dictionary = troll_column_dictionary ) # dplyr::glimpse(dat_rename) missing_dictionary_row <- troll_column_dictionary[-5,] # Drops "pH_units"  # The error when a column is missing from dictionary dat_rename <- TROLL_rename_cols(   df = dat_read,   colname_dictionary = missing_dictionary_row ) #> Error in apply_trollname_schema(normalized_df, dictionary = colname_dictionary, : Unknown column(s) detected: #>   - pH (pH) #>  #> Update `troll_column_dictionary` if these are valid new sensors.  new_row <- troll_column_dictionary[5,] new_row #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   pattern         canonical required meta  core_param stbl_calc #>   <chr>           <chr>     <lgl>    <lgl> <lgl>      <lgl>     #> 1 "^pH \\(pH\\)$" pH_units  FALSE    FALSE TRUE       TRUE good_dictionary <- missing_dictionary_row |>   dplyr::add_row(new_row)  # Apply updated dictionary dat_rename <- TROLL_rename_cols(   df = dat_read,   colname_dictionary = good_dictionary )  names(dat_rename) #>  [1] "DateTime"             "sp_conductivity_uScm" "temperature_C"        #>  [4] "pH_units"             "DO_mgL"               "DO_per"               #>  [7] "chlorophyll_RFU"      "turbidity_NTU"        "bga_fluorescence_RFU" #> [10] "depth_m"              "pH_mV"                "depth_to_water_m" troll_column_dictionary[troll_column_dictionary$meta,] #> # A tibble: 9 × 6 #>   pattern                          canonical required meta  core_param stbl_calc #>   <chr>                            <chr>     <lgl>    <lgl> <lgl>      <lgl>     #> 1 "^External Voltage"              external… FALSE    TRUE  FALSE      FALSE     #> 2 "^Battery Capacity"              battery_… FALSE    TRUE  FALSE      FALSE     #> 3 "^Pressure \\(psi\\)"            water_pr… FALSE    TRUE  FALSE      FALSE     #> 4 "^Barometric Pressure \\(mm Hg\… barometr… FALSE    TRUE  FALSE      FALSE     #> 5 "^Barometric Pressure \\(mbar\\… barometr… FALSE    TRUE  FALSE      FALSE     #> 6 "^Latitude"                      latitude… FALSE    TRUE  FALSE      FALSE     #> 7 "^Longitude"                     longitud… FALSE    TRUE  FALSE      FALSE     #> 8 "^Marked$"                       marked_f… FALSE    TRUE  FALSE      FALSE     #> 9 "^Trollcom_temperature_C$"       Trollcom… FALSE    TRUE  FALSE      FALSE  dat_meta <- TROLL_rename_cols(   df = dat_read,   strip_metadata = FALSE # Retain metadata columns ) names(dat_meta) #>  [1] "DateTime"                 "sp_conductivity_uScm"     #>  [3] "temperature_C"            "pH_units"                 #>  [5] "DO_mgL"                   "DO_per"                   #>  [7] "chlorophyll_RFU"          "turbidity_NTU"            #>  [9] "bga_fluorescence_RFU"     "depth_m"                  #> [11] "pH_mV"                    "depth_to_water_m"         #> [13] "external_voltage_V"       "battery_capacity_per"     #> [15] "barometric_pressure_mbar" "Trollcom_temperature_C"   #> [17] "latitude_deg"             "longitude_deg"            #> [19] "marked_flag" dat_echo <- TROLL_rename_cols(   df = dat_read,   verbose = TRUE ) #> Schema validation passed: 19 columns recognized. #> The CSV has Columns: #>   - DateTime #>   - sp_conductivity_uScm #>   - temperature_C #>   - pH_units #>   - DO_mgL #>   - DO_per #>   - chlorophyll_RFU #>   - turbidity_NTU #>   - bga_fluorescence_RFU #>   - depth_m #>   - pH_mV #>   - depth_to_water_m

what: Step 3: Detect Stationary Periods in Sonde Depth Data
title: TROLL Profiles Quick Start
text: naming schema standardized, next step detect periods sonde stationary water column. is_stationary function calculates right-justified (backwards looking) rolling range across window, assigns is_stationary_status based amount time sonde remains within depth_range_threshold. stationary periods detected, is_stationary appends stationary_dapth mean stationary observation depths. NOTE “Warning: Inconsistent sampling intervals…” created internal helper extracts sampling interval DateTime column. ’s telling inconsistent temporal “jumps” data, usually caused Troll-Comm glitching. issue “Dominant interval” detected (2 seconds example correct). 3 critical inputs tuning function: is_stationary_status critical tuning. - 999 flag indicates stationary block longer stationary_secs. - Periods shorter stationary_secs flagged duration seconds. visualize , toggle optional plotting: Note number stationary periods labelled values < 999, equal number seconds stationary period . Also note dashed lines. equate extracted stationary_depths used downstream stability determinations. Depending depths targeted, can adjust critical parameters mark additional depths stationary:  Now flagged stationary depth 0 - 8 meters 1.0 m intervals. Note function extracts actual depth mean stationary depths.
code: dat_stationary <- is_stationary(   df = dat_rename,   depth_col = depth_m,   datetime_col = DateTime,   depth_range_threshold = 0.05,   stationary_secs = 60,   rolling_range_secs = 10,   drop_cols = TRUE,   plot = FALSE ) #> Warning: Inconsistent sampling intervals detected. #> Dominant interval: 2 secs (99.2% of records) used for `samp_int`. #> Interval distribution: #>  sampling_interval   n #>                  2 263 #>                 26   1 #>                 66   1  # dplyr::glimpse(dat_stationary) 1) `depth_range_threshold`: the allowable rolling range that triggers the right-most observation of the rolling window to be flagged as stationary. 2) `stationary_secs`: the minimum time required for a stationary block to be flagged with `is_stationary_status` == 999. 3) `rolling_range_secs`: sets the rolling range window size. dat_stationary <- is_stationary(   df = dat_rename,   depth_range_threshold = 0.05,   stationary_secs = 45,    # Relatively long required stationary period   rolling_range_secs = 10,   plot = TRUE # Toggle optional plotting ) #> Warning: Inconsistent sampling intervals detected. #> Dominant interval: 2 secs (99.2% of records) used for `samp_int`. #> Interval distribution: #>  sampling_interval   n #>                  2 263 #>                 26   1 #>                 66   1 #> Warning: Removed 4 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_line()`). dat_stationary <- is_stationary(   df = dat_rename,   depth_range_threshold = 0.05,   stationary_secs = 20, # reduce required stationary time   rolling_range_secs = 10,   plot = TRUE # Toggle optional plotting ) #> Warning: Inconsistent sampling intervals detected. #> Dominant interval: 2 secs (99.2% of records) used for `samp_int`. #> Interval distribution: #>  sampling_interval   n #>                  2 263 #>                 26   1 #>                 66   1 #> Warning: Removed 4 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_line()`).

what: Step 4: Detect Stable Sensor Data Inside Stationary Blocks
title: TROLL Profiles Quick Start
text: NOTE function handles one column sensor data time. iteratively applied accross parameter column within TROLL_profile_compiler. Individual sensors behave inconsistently field data. order extract best possible final value, TROLL_profile_compiler uses range slope across stationary blocks data evaluate data stable, using maximum number observations meet criteria calculate summary statistic. Note function requires is_stationary_status appended data is_stationary. individual parameter:  optional plot aids understanding function . First, across entire stationary block data range slope calculated. , observations dropped iteratively one time slope_thresh range_thresh met. remaining observations stationary block marked <value_col>_stable == TRUE. Note use time x-axis. Also - two solid lines indicate range_thresh input user. guide visualization . Critical inputs :: Note stationary_status must 999 is_stationary() stability examined. depths missing consider tuning inputs is_stationary extract fully stationary blocks.
code: dat_stable <- TROLL_sensor_stable(   df = dat_stationary,   value_col = pH_units,   settling_secs = 10,   min_median_secs = 5,   slope_thresh = 0.05,   range_thresh = 0.02,   drop_cols = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE,   plot = TRUE           # Optional plotting toggled ON ) dat_stable |>     dplyr::select(DateTime, stationary_depth,is_stationary_status,pH_units_stable) #> # A tibble: 266 × 4 #>    DateTime            stationary_depth is_stationary_status pH_units_stable #>    <dttm>                         <dbl>                <dbl> <lgl>           #>  1 2025-05-13 15:44:49           NA                        0 NA              #>  2 2025-05-13 15:44:51            0.085                  888 NA              #>  3 2025-05-13 15:44:53            0.085                  888 NA              #>  4 2025-05-13 15:44:55            0.085                  888 NA              #>  5 2025-05-13 15:44:57            0.085                  888 NA              #>  6 2025-05-13 15:44:59            0.085                  888 NA              #>  7 2025-05-13 15:45:01            0.085                  999 FALSE           #>  8 2025-05-13 15:45:03            0.085                  999 FALSE           #>  9 2025-05-13 15:45:05            0.085                  999 FALSE           #> 10 2025-05-13 15:45:07            0.085                  999 FALSE           #> # ℹ 256 more rows 1) `slope_thresh`: the slope threshold that must be met for stability.  2) `range_thresh`: the data range threshold that must be met for stability.  3) `min_secs`: Minimum duration of data to be retained regardless if `slope_thresh` and `range_thresh` are met.

title: TROLL Profiles Quick Start
text: data flagged stable hand, TROLL_stable_summary function compiles output single value (default = median) stationary depth:
code: dat_summary <- TROLL_stable_summary(   df = dat_stable,   group_col = stationary_depth ) dat_summary #> # A tibble: 9 × 2 #>   stationary_depth pH_units #>              <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1            0.085     7.26 #> 2            1.04      7.41 #> 3            1.94      7.33 #> 4            2.9       7.00 #> 5            3.91      6.74 #> 6            4.89      6.53 #> 7            5.84      6.36 #> 8            6.83      6.12 #> 9            7.68      5.84

title: TROLL Profiles Quick Start
text: steps may executed using TROLL_profile_compiler function. high level wrapper automatically detects parameters using troll_colname_dictionary iterates TROLL_sensor_stable provide unique stability flags paramter. Default values individualrange_thresh parameter given stability_ranges: may updated passing named numeric vector summarize_data FALSE, output just return data “_stable” flag column parameter. TRUE list returned flagged summary data: optional plotting used visually ensure parameter compiled expected. plots classical view profile data, data values x axis depth y-axis.  individual parameter looks incorrect, recommended move outside TROLL_profile_compiler work individual workflow tune, bring values back wrapper.
code: #> # A tibble: 8 × 3 #>   param                range slope #>   <chr>                <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 sp_conductivity_uScm  0.5   1    #> 2 temperature_C         0.15  0.25 #> 3 pH_units              0.1   0.05 #> 4 DO_mgL                0.15  0.15 #> 5 turbidity_NTU         4     0.5  #> 6 chlorophyll_RFU       1     0.5  #> 7 bga_fluorescence_RFU  1     0.5  #> 8 ORP_mv               10     1 custom_ranges <- c('DO_mgL' = 0.2, 'pH_units' = 0.15)  dat_final <- TROLL_profile_compiler(   path = path,   depth_col = depth_m,   datetime_col = DateTime,   stn_depthrange = 0.05,   stn_secs = 24,                                  # Note use of value from tuning above   stn_rollwindow_secs = 10,   stbl_min_secs = 5,   stbl_settle_secs = 10,   stbl_range_thresholds = custom_ranges,           # Provide updated custom ranges   summarize_data = TRUE,                           # Output will be a list w/ both flagged and summary data   drop_cols = TRUE,   plot = c(Final = FALSE, Stationary = FALSE) ) #> Warning: Inconsistent sampling intervals detected. #> Dominant interval: 2 secs (99.2% of records) used for `samp_int`. #> Interval distribution: #>  sampling_interval   n #>                  2 263 #>                 26   1 #>                 66   1 #> Warning in TROLL_sensor_stable(df = dat_stationary, value_col = !!param_i, : turbidity_NTU slope exceeded threshold at: #>   2.9 m (0.716 units/min) #>   6.83 m (-0.554 units/min) #>   7.68 m (-1.673 units/min) #>  #> Consider validating data or adjusting trimming. flag_data <- dat_final$Flagged_Data summary <- dat_final$Summary_Data  summary #> # A tibble: 9 × 8 #>   stationary_depth sp_conductivity_uScm temperature_C pH_units DO_mgL #>              <dbl>                <dbl>         <dbl>    <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1            0.085                 66.8         18.0      7.26   9.85 #> 2            1.04                  66.6         18.1      7.41   9.86 #> 3            1.94                  66.3         17.5      7.33   9.68 #> 4            2.9                   66.7         16.1      7.00   9.11 #> 5            3.91                  65.7         15.3      6.74   8.02 #> 6            4.89                  67.2         12.7      6.53   6.43 #> 7            5.84                  66.9         10.6      6.36   5.08 #> 8            6.83                  67.0          9.83     6.12   4.05 #> 9            7.68                  68.9          9.52     5.84   2.38 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: chlorophyll_RFU <dbl>, turbidity_NTU <dbl>, #> #   bga_fluorescence_RFU <dbl> #> Warning: Inconsistent sampling intervals detected. #> Dominant interval: 2 secs (99.2% of records) used for `samp_int`. #> Interval distribution: #>  sampling_interval   n #>                  2 263 #>                 26   1 #>                 66   1 #> Warning in TROLL_sensor_stable(df = dat_stationary, value_col = !!param_i, : turbidity_NTU slope exceeded threshold at: #>   2.9 m (0.676 units/min) #>   7.68 m (-1.673 units/min) #>  #> Consider validating data or adjusting trimming.

title: Tuning TROLL Workflow
text: AquaTROLL utility functions ImportUtils provide tools processing electronic logs generated VuSitu app. primary workhorse TROLL_profile_compiler(), wraps functionality supporting functions single interface batch processing. Ideally, function required process raw AquaTroll data (see TROLL-QuickStart vignette). event error triggered, output expected, component function used within TROLL_profile_compiler() also exported individually, allowing users inspect intermediate steps, troubleshoot issues, better understand data transformed throughout workflow. workflow : Read Raw Data (TROLL_read_data()) Standardize column names required data (TROLL_rename_cols()) Detect flag stationary periods sonde data (is_stationary()) Detect flag stable data sensor type (TROLL_sensor_stable()) Summarize stationary + stable data (TROLL_stable_summary()) functions assume raw VuSitu .html files already converted .csv format. vignette walks complete example workflow using individual functions, followed wrapper function. goal demonstrate purpose step help users make informed decisions efficient accurate data processing.

title: Tuning TROLL Workflow
text: TROLL_read_data() function reads raw .csv files exported VuSitu. uses pattern matching locate “Date Time” column determine data begin. function checks whether seconds included datetime values attempts standardize formatting needed. “Date Time” column used identify start dataset, must present input file. stage, data imported without interpretation modification beyond basic formatting (e.g Date Time Depth (m) column formatting enforced).
code: dat_read <- TROLL_read_data(path = file_location_path)

title: Tuning TROLL Workflow
text: Downstream functions rely consistent column naming; function enforces standard (canonical) naming conventions via troll_column_dictionary.
code: dat_rename <- TROLL_rename_cols(   df = dat_read,   colname_dictionary = troll_column_dictionary )

what: If you see an error about “Unknown Columns …”
title: Tuning TROLL Workflow
text: Column renaming achieved pattern matching. unknown columns detected error displayed indicating dictionary updated columns represent new sensor data. However, possible work around missing names creating unique dictionary object passing colname_dictionary argument using troll_column_dictionary template adding additional rows required. Columns associated TROLL COMM automatically detected using internal dataset TROLL COMM serial numbers. TROLL COMM temperature column renamed avoid conflicts water temperature. COMM columns stripped away metadata whent strip_metadata = TRUE. trollcomm_serials arguments allows users use custom vector serial numbers associated TROLL COMMunits, internal dataset date. Metadata columns (e.g. “latitude”, “longitude”, “battery” etc.) removed default, can retained toggling strip_metadata == FALSE. inclusive list metadata columns defined within column dictionary: troubleshooting, verbose argument may toggled allow printing messages console regarding presence/absense Troll-Comm columns, parameters detected.
code: # Drop "pH_units" from the dictionary to simulate a missing parameter missing_dictionary_row <- troll_column_dictionary[-5,]   # With "pH_units" missing, an ERROR is shown: dat_rename <- TROLL_rename_cols(   df = dat_read,   colname_dictionary = missing_dictionary_row ) #> Error in apply_trollname_schema(normalized_df, dictionary = colname_dictionary, : Unknown column(s) detected: #>   - pH (pH) #>  #> Update `troll_column_dictionary` if these are valid new sensors. # Create a new row with the missing information, formatted appropriately new_row <- troll_column_dictionary[5,] new_row #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   pattern         canonical required meta  core_param stbl_calc #>   <chr>           <chr>     <lgl>    <lgl> <lgl>      <lgl>     #> 1 "^pH \\(pH\\)$" pH_units  FALSE    FALSE TRUE       TRUE  # Add to the dictionary object good_dictionary <- missing_dictionary_row |>   dplyr::add_row(new_row)  # Apply updated dictionary dat_rename <- TROLL_rename_cols(   df = dat_read,   colname_dictionary = good_dictionary )  names(dat_rename) #>  [1] "DateTime"             "sp_conductivity_uScm" "temperature_C"        #>  [4] "pH_units"             "DO_mgL"               "DO_per"               #>  [7] "chlorophyll_RFU"      "turbidity_NTU"        "bga_fluorescence_RFU" #> [10] "depth_m"              "pH_mV"                "depth_to_water_m" troll_column_dictionary[troll_column_dictionary$meta,] #> # A tibble: 9 × 6 #>   pattern                          canonical required meta  core_param stbl_calc #>   <chr>                            <chr>     <lgl>    <lgl> <lgl>      <lgl>     #> 1 "^External Voltage"              external… FALSE    TRUE  FALSE      FALSE     #> 2 "^Battery Capacity"              battery_… FALSE    TRUE  FALSE      FALSE     #> 3 "^Pressure \\(psi\\)"            water_pr… FALSE    TRUE  FALSE      FALSE     #> 4 "^Barometric Pressure \\(mm Hg\… barometr… FALSE    TRUE  FALSE      FALSE     #> 5 "^Barometric Pressure \\(mbar\\… barometr… FALSE    TRUE  FALSE      FALSE     #> 6 "^Latitude"                      latitude… FALSE    TRUE  FALSE      FALSE     #> 7 "^Longitude"                     longitud… FALSE    TRUE  FALSE      FALSE     #> 8 "^Marked$"                       marked_f… FALSE    TRUE  FALSE      FALSE     #> 9 "^Trollcom_temperature_C$"       Trollcom… FALSE    TRUE  FALSE      FALSE  dat_meta <- TROLL_rename_cols(   df = dat_read,   strip_metadata = FALSE # Retain metadata columns ) dat_echo <- TROLL_rename_cols(   df = dat_read,   verbose = TRUE ) #> Schema validation passed: 19 columns recognized. #> The CSV has Columns: #>   - DateTime #>   - sp_conductivity_uScm #>   - temperature_C #>   - pH_units #>   - DO_mgL #>   - DO_per #>   - chlorophyll_RFU #>   - turbidity_NTU #>   - bga_fluorescence_RFU #>   - depth_m #>   - pH_mV #>   - depth_to_water_m

what: Step 3: Detect Stationary Periods in Sonde Depth Data
title: Tuning TROLL Workflow
text: naming schema standardized, next step detect periods sonde stationary water column. can simply pass path df argument is_stationary steps 1 & 2 completed within function is_stationary function detects periods sonde stationary based rolling range, assigns is_stationary_status based amount time sonde remained within depth_range_threshold.

previous_headings: Step 3: Detect Stationary Periods in Sonde Depth Data
title: Tuning TROLL Workflow
text: three parameters control stationary periods identified: smaller = stricter larger = persmissive (risk false positives) Larger values = long duration pauses retained Smaller values = shorter pauses included Larger values = smoother, conservative detection Smaller values = sensitive short-term variation Figure 1: Plot output is_stationary indicating stationary depths extracted (horizontal dashed lines top panel). stationary periods detected, is_stationary appends stationary_depthas mean stationary observation depths input df. can see legend, stationary periods longer stationary_secs flagged 999, indicating fully stationary. critical downstream use, is_stationary_status 999 observations considered stability detection. example, can see bottom panel Figure 1, depth_range_threshold strict, causing splitting blocks hover near depth. Figure 2: Plot output is_stationary adjustment depth_range_threshold Now, blocks continuous, clear want include data > 5 m, relax stationary_secs allow shorter periods . use stationary_secs = 24, blocks ~ 8.0 m included. Figure 3: Plot output form is_stationary adjustment stationary_secs. “Warning: Inconsistent sampling intervals…” created internal helper extracts sampling interval DateTime column. ’s telling inconsistent temporal “jumps” data, usually caused Troll-Comm glitching. issue “Dominant Interval” detected (2 seconds example correct). Stationary periods calculated based time, large jumps problem long sonde held position.
code: dat_stationary <-  is_stationary(df = dat_rename,               depth_range_threshold = 0.025, # Default = 0.05               stationary_secs = 40,          # Default = 45               rolling_range_secs = 10,       # Default = 10               plot = TRUE) dat_stationary <-  is_stationary(df = dat_rename,               depth_range_threshold = 0.05, # increased to avoid erroneous splits (this is the default value)               stationary_secs = 40,               rolling_range_secs = 10,               plot = TRUE) dat_stationary <-  is_stationary(df = dat_rename,               depth_range_threshold = 0.05, # increased to avoid erroneous splits (this is the default value)               stationary_secs = 24,               plot = TRUE) #> Warning: Inconsistent sampling intervals detected. #> Dominant interval: 2 secs (99.2% of records) used for `samp_int`. #> Interval distribution: #>  sampling_interval   n #>                  2 263 #>                 26   1 #>                 66   1 #> Warning: Removed 4 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_line()`).

what: Step 4: Detect Stable Sensor Data Inside Stationary Blocks
title: Tuning TROLL Workflow
text: TROLL_sensor_stable() function handles one column sensor data time. iteratively applied across parameter columns within TROLL_profile_compiler using default range slope threshold values (stability_ranges). section, function applied outside compiler demonstrate tuning TROLL_sensor_stable may applied individual parameters, necessary.

previous_headings: Step 4: Detect Stable Sensor Data Inside Stationary Blocks
title: Tuning TROLL Workflow
text: core, stability detection functions across data parameters: Stationary blocks data identified using is_stationary initial “settling period” trimmed start block via settling_secs Slope range calculated across stationary block independently Observations dropped iteratively start stationary block, slope range re-calculated remainder min_secs time remains stationary block slope_thresh range_thresh met, remainder block used calculate median value Optical Probes (Turbidity, Chlorophyll, BGA) utilize “stationary” data trimming stationary_secs due optical sensor behavior. warning shown slope threshold exceeded one optical parameters, allowing user explore suspect data specific stationary depth output TROLL_stable_summary.

previous_headings: Step 4: Detect Stable Sensor Data Inside Stationary Blocks
title: Tuning TROLL Workflow
text: may intuitive first view water column profile data time series plots, however, user able visually assess parameter values changing cast relative stationary blocks data, whether extracted “stable” data points representative reality. Figure 4: Plot output TROLL_sensor_stable pH. horizontal dashed lines represent range_thresh value, can useful tuning. dark purple points extracted “stable” data used calculate median stationary depth TROLL_stable_summary. Figure 4 exponential type relationship apparent across stationary block data, stability occurring late block, . Due nature parameters measured, well optical sensors record data, values optical sensors (turbidity, chlorophyll, BGA) tend noisier exhibit exponential changes (Figure 5). Figure 5: Plot output TROLL_sensor_stable turbidity. function warns us slope threshold exceeded 3 stationary depths: 2.90 m (fourth stationary block accounting depth ~ 0) clearly caused small number observations tail stationary block ~ 2.4 - 2.5 NTU 6.83 m data exhibits consistent drift negative direction 7.68 m primarily result higher NTU values (relative basis percent change appear less impactful) range threshold lines Figure 5 used optical sensors.
code: dat_stable <- TROLL_sensor_stable(   df = dat_stationary,   value_col = pH_units,   settling_secs = 10,  # Front end trimming of stationary blocks   min_median_secs = 5, # Minimum amount of data retained to calculate median if stability is not detected   slope_thresh = 0.05,   range_thresh = 0.02,   plot = TRUE           # Optional plotting toggled ON ) dat_stable <- TROLL_sensor_stable(   df = dat_stationary,   value_col = turbidity_NTU,   settling_secs = 10,  # Front end trimming of stationary blocks   min_median_secs = 5, # Minimum amount of data retained to calculate median if stability is not detected   slope_thresh = NULL, # Use defaults   range_thresh = NULL, # Use defaults   plot = TRUE          # Optional plotting toggled ON ) #> Warning in TROLL_sensor_stable(df = dat_stationary, value_col = turbidity_NTU, : turbidity_NTU slope exceeded threshold at: #>   2.9 m (0.716 units/min) #>   6.83 m (-0.554 units/min) #>   7.68 m (-1.673 units/min) #>  #> Consider validating data or adjusting trimming.

title: Tuning TROLL Workflow
text: data flagged stable hand, TROLL_stable_summary function compiles output single value (default = median) stationary depth. carried internally TROLL_profile_compiler summarize_data set TRUE. demonstration compile summary data turbidity used Figure 5.
code: dat_summary <- TROLL_stable_summary(   df = dat_stable,   group_col = stationary_depth ) |>   dplyr::select(stationary_depth, turbidity_NTU) dat_summary #> # A tibble: 9 × 2 #>   stationary_depth turbidity_NTU #>              <dbl>         <dbl> #> 1            0.085         0.928 #> 2            1.04          0.950 #> 3            1.94          1.08  #> 4            2.9           0.987 #> 5            3.91          0.912 #> 6            4.89          0.860 #> 7            5.84          1.45  #> 8            6.83          3.36  #> 9            7.68          9.58

title: Tuning TROLL Workflow
text: TROLL_profile_compiler function high level wrapper automatically detects parameters using troll_colname_dictionary iterates TROLL_sensor_stable provide summarized data entire AquaTroll sonde cast. Inputs compiler named indicate package functions relate : prefix “stn_” associated inputs is_stationary prefix “stbl_” associated input TROLL_sensor_stable output compiler either: list length (2) summarize_data set TRUE first list element named “Flagged_Data”, raw input dataframe “_stable” flag appended parameter column. second list element summary output TROLL_stable_summary. Instead timeseries plots, compiler function outputs classical “profile” plots core parameters data plot = c(Final = TRUE).

what: Updating Inputs to the Compiler Based on Tuning Results
title: Tuning TROLL Workflow
text: default values inputs needs tuned, can updated: Table 1: Input naming conventions among package functions. Default values individual range_thresh slope_thresh parameter given stability_ranges: may updated passing named numeric vector. want relax range_thresh dissolved oxygen pH example: Currently updating slope thresholds allowed summarize_data FALSE, output just return raw dataframe “_stable” flag column parameter. Flagged_Data always returned, Summary_Data can extracted output list summarize_data = TRUE: Table 2: Example $Summary_Data compiler function output. optional plotting used visually ensure parameter compiled expected. plots shown classical view profile data, data values x axis depth y-axis. Optional plotting toggled plot = c(Final = TRUE), plot = c(Final = TRUE, Stationary = TRUE) (show stationary plot like Figure 3), plot = TRUE (toggles plots ). individual parameter looks incorrect, recommended move outside TROLL_profile_compiler work individual workflow tune, bring values back wrapper.
code: #> # A tibble: 8 × 3 #>   param                range slope #>   <chr>                <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 sp_conductivity_uScm  0.5   1    #> 2 temperature_C         0.15  0.25 #> 3 pH_units              0.1   0.05 #> 4 DO_mgL                0.15  0.15 #> 5 turbidity_NTU         4     0.5  #> 6 chlorophyll_RFU       1     0.5  #> 7 bga_fluorescence_RFU  1     0.5  #> 8 ORP_mv               10     1 # Create a named vector of new range threshold values custom_ranges <- c('DO_mgL' = 0.2, 'pH_units' = 0.15)  dat_final <- TROLL_profile_compiler(   path = file_location_path,   stn_depthrange = 0.05,   stn_secs = 24,                                   # Note use of value from tuning above   stbl_range_thresholds = custom_ranges,           # Provide updated custom ranges   summarize_data = TRUE,                           # Output will be a list w/ both flagged and summary data   plot = c(Final = FALSE, Stationary = FALSE) ) flag_data <- dat_final$Flagged_Data summary <- dat_final$Summary_Data

what: Identify Stationary Periods in Depth Data — is_stationary
title: Identify Stationary Periods in Depth Data — is_stationary
text: function flags periods sonde depth sensor effectively stationary based rolling range depth measurements. can handle irregular sampling intervals, optionally trims start stationary blocks, optionally plots depth rolling range time.

title: Identify Stationary Periods in Depth Data — is_stationary
code: is_stationary(   df,   depth_col = depth_m,   datetime_col = DateTime,   depth_range_threshold = 0.05,   stationary_secs = 60,   rolling_range_secs = 10,   drop_cols = TRUE,   plot = FALSE )

title: Identify Stationary Periods in Depth Data — is_stationary
text: df data frame containing depth datetime observations. Optionally, path may passed location raw TROLL CSV file, case `TROLL_read_data()` `TROLL_rename_cols()` run default setting create `df`.. depth_col Column `df` representing depth measurements (numeric). Default `depth_m`. datetime_col Column `df` representing observation timestamps (POSIXt). Default `DateTime`. depth_range_threshold Numeric. Maximum depth range within rolling window consider sonde stationary. Default `0.05`. stationary_secs Numeric. Minimum duration seconds block considered stationary. Default `60`. rolling_range_secs Numeric. Window size seconds used calculate rolling depth range. Default `10`. drop_cols Logical. TRUE, intermediate columns used computation removed output. Default `TRUE`. plot Logical. TRUE, produces plots showing depth rolling range stationary periods highlighted. Default `FALSE`.

title: Identify Stationary Periods in Depth Data — is_stationary
text: data frame identical `df` additional columns (provided `drop_cols` TRUE:  is_stationary_status Numeric. 999 = fully stationary block, intermediate values = partial stationary duration seconds, 0 = stationary. stationary_depth Mean depth stationary blocks (NA stationary). `drop_cols` FALSE, intermediate columns retained addition is_stationary_status, stationary_depth:  is_stationary_initial Logical. TRUE = met rolling range criteria `start_trim_secs` applied, prior application `stationary_secs`. stationary_block_id Numeric. Consecutive id's data blocks, including non-stationary blocks block_n Numeric. number observations stationary block block_secs Numeric. Elapsed time seconds stationary block.

title: Identify Stationary Periods in Depth Data — is_stationary
text: function first calculates sampling interval based lagged differences `datetime_col`. computes rolling range depth values across window size length = `rolling_range_secs`. identifies candidate stationary periods rolling range `depth_range_threshold`. Blocks shorter `stationary_secs` flagged `is_stationary_status` equal seconds stationary. Blocks longer `stationary_secs` flagged 999. sampling interval exceeds 30 seconds, sonde assumed fixed, rows returned `is_stationary_status = 999`.

title: Identify Stationary Periods in Depth Data — is_stationary
code: if (FALSE) { library(dplyr) df <- data.frame( DateTime = seq.POSIXt(from = as.POSIXct("2026-01-01 00:00"),                       by = "sec", length.out = 170), depth_m = c(rep(1.0, 50), rep(1.05, 70), rnorm(50,2,.02)) ) df_flagged <- is_stationary(df, depth_col = depth_m, datetime_col = DateTime,                             start_trim_secs = 5, stationary_secs = 45,                             depth_range_threshold = 0.05) }

Name logical column indicating stability (typically "<param>_stable"). range_thresh Numeric. Range threshold used stability detection. NA, threshold lines drawn.

title: Plot Sensor Stability Diagnostics — plot_stability
text: plot printed.

title: Plot Sensor Stability Diagnostics — plot_stability
text: Observations colored profiling state: "Sonde Moving": Sonde motion "Unstable Stationary": Stationary stability criteria met "Stable Stationary": Meets stability criteria "Trimmed (Settling)": Removed analysis due settling time range_thresh provided, horizontal dashed lines drawn around median value visualize allowable range threshold. function primarily intended diagnostic visualization tuning stability parameters.

title: Default Range Values For Flagging Sensor Stability — stability_ranges

what: Aqua TROLL Column Mapping and Metadata Dictionary — troll_column_dictionary
title: Aqua TROLL Column Mapping and Metadata Dictionary — troll_column_dictionary
text: data dictionary used map raw -Situ Aqua TROLL sensor output headers standardized (canonical) variable names. also contains metadata flags used internal functions identify core parameters, instrument metadata, variables eligible stability calculations.

title: Aqua TROLL Column Mapping and Metadata Dictionary — troll_column_dictionary
code: troll_column_dictionary

title: Aqua TROLL Column Mapping and Metadata Dictionary — troll_column_dictionary
text: ## `troll_column_dictionary` tibble `r nrow(troll_column_dictionary)` rows 6 columns: pattern Character. Regex pattern used match raw column headers   TROLL CSV/text exports. canonical Character. standardized snake_case name used   within package. required Logical. TRUE, columns must present   basic processing. meta Logical. Indicates column represents instrument   diagnostics (e.g., battery, voltage) rather environmental data. core_param Logical. Indicates column primary   water quality parameter. stbl_calc Logical. Indicates parameter eligible   sensor stability flagging TROLL_sensor_stable().

title: Aqua TROLL Column Mapping and Metadata Dictionary — troll_column_dictionary
code: # View all core parameters subset(troll_column_dictionary, core_param == TRUE) #> # A tibble: 9 × 6 #>   pattern                       canonical    required meta  core_param stbl_calc #>   <chr>                         <chr>        <lgl>    <lgl> <lgl>      <lgl>     #> 1 "^Specific Conductivity"      sp_conducti… FALSE    FALSE TRUE       TRUE      #> 2 "^Temperature \\(°C\\)$"      temperature… FALSE    FALSE TRUE       TRUE      #> 3 "^pH \\(pH\\)$"               pH_units     FALSE    FALSE TRUE       TRUE      #> 4 "^RDO Concentration"          DO_mgL       FALSE    FALSE TRUE       TRUE      #> 5 "^RDO Saturation"             DO_per       FALSE    FALSE TRUE       FALSE     #> 6 "^Turbidity"                  turbidity_N… FALSE    FALSE TRUE       TRUE      #> 7 "^Chlorophyll-a Fluorescence" chlorophyll… FALSE    FALSE TRUE       TRUE      #> 8 "^BGA-PC Fluorescence"        bga_fluores… FALSE    FALSE TRUE       TRUE      #> 9 "^ORP"                        ORP_mv       FALSE    FALSE TRUE       TRUE

what: Bulk Process TROLL Sonde Profiles — TROLL_profile_compiler
title: Bulk Process TROLL Sonde Profiles — TROLL_profile_compiler
text: TROLL_profile_compiler() reads raw TROLL sonde data, identifies stationary periods, evaluates sensor stability parameter, optionally summarizes results, provides optional plotting raw final summarized data.

title: Bulk Process TROLL Sonde Profiles — TROLL_profile_compiler
code: TROLL_profile_compiler(   path,   depth_col = depth_m,   datetime_col = DateTime,   stn_depthrange = 0.1,   stn_secs = 45,   stn_rollwindow_secs = 10,   stbl_settle_secs = 10,   stbl_min_secs = 5,   stbl_range_thresholds = NULL,   summarize_data = TRUE,   drop_cols = TRUE,   plot = c(Final = FALSE, Stationary = FALSE),   debug = FALSE )

title: Bulk Process TROLL Sonde Profiles — TROLL_profile_compiler
text: path Character. Path raw TROLL CSV file. depth_col Unquoted column name water depth (numeric data). datetime_col Unquoted column name date-time (POSIXct, POSIXt, Date). stn_depthrange Numeric. Depth range threshold is_stationary(). stn_secs Numeric. Minimum time (seconds) required stationary block considered fully stationary (flagged : is_stationary_status = 999). stn_rollwindow_secs Numeric. Window size (seconds) used compute rolling range stationary detection. stbl_settle_secs Numeric. Seconds trim start stationary block. stbl_min_secs Numeric. Minimum seconds used calculate summary statistic stability reached within stationary period. stbl_range_thresholds Optional named numeric vector. Custom stability thresholds per parameter. See stability_ranges defaults naming. summarize_data Logical. TRUE, returns `$Flagged_Data` `$Summary_Data` (medians) list. drop_cols Logical. TRUE, removes intermediate processing columns. plot Logical named logical vector. Controls optional plotting is_stationary TROLL_stable_summary. Default c(Final = FALSE, Stationary = FALSE). debug Logical. TRUE, prints parameters looped stability calculation aid debugging.

title: Bulk Process TROLL Sonde Profiles — TROLL_profile_compiler
text: output depends summarize_data argument: summarize_data = FALSE Returns data.frame   original sensor data appended _stable logical flags. summarize_data = TRUE Returns list containing: Flagged_Data: full data frame stability flags. Summary_Data: summarized tibble median sensor       readings stable stationary period.

title: Bulk Process TROLL Sonde Profiles — TROLL_profile_compiler
text: function executes TROLL processing workflow following order: Ingestion & Cleanup: Reads data via TROLL_read_data()   standardizes names via TROLL_rename_cols(). Stationary Detection: Uses is_stationary() identify   depths sonde held steady. Arguments step   prefixed stn_. Stability Evaluation: Calls TROLL_sensor_stable()   parameter identify "equilibrated" data points. Arguments   step prefixed stbl_. Core parameters data   automatically detected : troll_column_dictionary[troll_column_dictionary$stbl_calc==TRUE,]. Summarization: requested, calculates median values stable   windows depth using TROLL_stable_summary(). Visualization: Optionally generates diagnostic plots comparing raw   profiles filtered stationary/stable points.

title: Bulk Process TROLL Sonde Profiles — TROLL_profile_compiler
code: if (FALSE) { # Standard profile compilation result <- TROLL_profile_compiler(   path = "extdata/sonde_file.csv",   depth_col = depth_m,   datetime_col = DateTime,   stn_depthrange = 0.1,   stn_secs = 45,   stbl_min_secs = 5,   plot = c(Final = TRUE, Stationary = FALSE) )  # Access results head(result$Flagged_Data) head(result$Summary_Data) }

title: Read Aqua-Troll Data File — TROLL_read_data
text: Reads raw TROLL data file automatically locates header row containing `"Date Time"`. function validates timestamp column exists ensures timestamps include **second-level resolution**. Supported timestamp formats `"ymd HMS"` `"mdy HMS"`.

title: Read Aqua-Troll Data File — TROLL_read_data
code: TROLL_read_data(path)

title: Read Aqua-Troll Data File — TROLL_read_data
text: path Character string. File path raw TROLL data file.

title: Read Aqua-Troll Data File — TROLL_read_data
text: tibble containing imported data original column structure raw file.

title: Read Aqua-Troll Data File — TROLL_read_data
text: function performs several validation checks: Confirms file exists specified path. Searches file row containing `"Date Time"` identify   start data table. Ensures exactly one header row containing `"Date Time"` exists. Imports data using readr::read_csv() starting   detected header row. Verifies timestamps `"Date Time"` column include seconds. timestamps contain minute resolution (e.g., `"ymd HM"` `"mdy HM"`), function error.

title: Standardize TROLL Data Column Names — TROLL_rename_cols
code: TROLL_rename_cols(   df,   trollcomm_serials = trollCOMM_serials,   colname_dictionary = troll_column_dictionary,   strip_metadata = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Standardize TROLL Data Column Names — TROLL_rename_cols
text: df data frame read raw TROLL CSV export. trollcomm_serials numeric vector known TROLL-COM serial numbers. Defaults internal trollCOMM_serials lookup. colname_dictionary Dataframe; default = `troll_column_dictionary`. Provided regex patterns associated canonical naming schema standardizing column names. strip_metadata Logical; TRUE, removes metadata columns specified `troll_column_dictionary`. verbose Logical; TRUE, prints final standardized column names console.

title: Standardize TROLL Data Column Names — TROLL_rename_cols
text: data frame validated standardized column names.

title: Standardize TROLL Data Column Names — TROLL_rename_cols
text: function performs following steps: Detects renames TROLL-COM temperature columns. Normalizes raw column names (removes serial numbers). Applies internal column names dictionary validation         canonical renaming. function stop execution : Unknown columns detected, Multiple columns map canonical name, Required columns missing.

what: Identify Stable Sensor Values Within Stationary Profiling Blocks — TROLL_sensor_stable
title: Identify Stable Sensor Values Within Stationary Profiling Blocks — TROLL_sensor_stable
text: Evaluates whether sensor measurements collected stationary profiling periods stable based slope range thresholds. Stability assessed within stationary block identified is_stationary(), function flags observations meet slope range criteria.

title: Identify Stable Sensor Values Within Stationary Profiling Blocks — TROLL_sensor_stable
code: TROLL_sensor_stable(   df,   value_col = pH_units,   min_median_secs = 4,   slope_thresh = NULL,   range_thresh = NULL,   settling_secs = 10,   drop_cols = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE,   plot = FALSE )

title: Identify Stable Sensor Values Within Stationary Profiling Blocks — TROLL_sensor_stable
text: df data frame containing sensor data stationary block metadata. value_col Unquoted name sensor column evaluate stability (e.g., pH_units, temp_C, sp_conductivity_uScm). min_median_secs Numeric. Minimum duration (seconds) data retain end stationary block. stability criteria met, trailing window still flagged stable used summary calculations. Default 5. slope_thresh Numeric. Maximum allowable absolute slope (units per minute) stable measurements. NULL, parameter-specific default retrieved stability_ranges. range_thresh Numeric. Maximum allowable range (max - min) within evaluation window. NULL, parameter-specific default retrieved stability_ranges. settling_secs Numeric. Number seconds remove start stationary block allow sensor equilibration. observations flagged is_stationary_status = 888. drop_cols Logical. TRUE (default), intermediate diagnostic columns used slope range calculations removed returned data frame. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints depths stationary blocks identified. plot Logical. TRUE, generates diagnostic plot using plot_stability(). Intended tuning debugging, final analysis outputs.

title: Identify Stable Sensor Values Within Stationary Profiling Blocks — TROLL_sensor_stable
text: data frame containing original input data added logical column <value_col>_stable. column indicates whether observation meets stability criteria. drop_cols = FALSE, additional diagnostic columns included: slope: slope evaluation window range: value range within window n_used: number observations used n_dropped: number leading observations removed slope_ok, range_ok: logical threshold checks

title: Identify Stable Sensor Values Within Stationary Profiling Blocks — TROLL_sensor_stable
text: stationary block, slope (units per minute) value range calculated iteratively using progressively smaller windows, beginning full stationary block removing leading observations. minimum tail duration defined min_median_secs always retained. function searches earliest point block slope_thresh range_thresh satisfied, flags observation subsequent observations stable. optical parameters (e.g., turbidity, chlorophyll, fluorescence), observations within stationary periods (settling removal) flagged stable. Range-based criteria applied, slope used generate diagnostic warnings. non-optical sensors, function searches `slope_thresh` `range_thresh` met concurrently, marks remainder stationary block "stable". neither threshold can met, final min_median_secs data within stationary block retained flagged stable. input data frame must contain following columns: DateTime depth_m stationary_depth stationary_block_id is_stationary_status column specified value_col Observations NA values value_col excluded stability calculations. function requires stationary blocks already identified using is_stationary.Stability calculations performed observations is_stationary_status == 999, .e., fully stationary data removal initial settling period. function error stationary block contain sufficient duration (settling removal) satisfy min_median_secs.

title: Identify Stable Sensor Values Within Stationary Profiling Blocks — TROLL_sensor_stable
code: if (FALSE) { dat <- is_stationary(sensor_data)  dat <- TROLL_sensor_stable(   df = dat,   value_col = pH_units,   min_median_secs = 5,   slope_thresh = 0.05,   range_thresh = 0.02 ) }

title: Summarize stable TROLL observations — TROLL_stable_summary
text: Summarizes sensor values using observations flagged stable (.e., corresponding `<sensor>_stable` columns `TRUE`).

title: Summarize stable TROLL observations — TROLL_stable_summary
code: TROLL_stable_summary(   df,   group_col = stationary_depth,   summary_fn = median,   digits = 4 )

title: Summarize stable TROLL observations — TROLL_stable_summary
text: df data frame containing sensor observations corresponding `_stable` flag columns. group_col Column used group observations (e.g., `stationary_depth`). summary_fn Summary function applied stable values. Default `median`. digits Numeric. Number decimal places round result `summary_fn`.

title: Summarize stable TROLL observations — TROLL_stable_summary
text: data frame one row per group summarized   sensor values parameter.

title: Summarize stable TROLL observations — TROLL_stable_summary
text: Intended create final values TROLL_profile_compiler function. function automatically detects data columns paired `_stable` flag columns computes summary statistic parameter within specified grouping variable.
